name: Test — dapr durability replay
description: Paced multi-tool workflow used to validate child agent workflow durability. Agent runs three separate bash commands with sleep between them so there's a reliable 30-60s window to kill a pod mid-run. Each bash call creates + appends a marker file. After replay the final file must contain every marker exactly once (activities cached by Dapr; not re-executed on replay).

document.name: test-durability-replay
document.title: Durability replay probe
document.summary: Three sequential bash steps with sleep padding; used for pod-kill replay testing.

input.promptPlaceholder: (optional) any input — the workflow is fully instructed

with.stopCondition: Stop only after trace.txt exists and contains exactly three lines matching marker-1, marker-2, marker-3 in order.
with.prompt:
Four-step paced task. Run each command separately and wait for its output before issuing the next:

1. Run `sleep 30 && echo marker-1 > trace.txt && cat trace.txt`
2. Run `sleep 30 && echo marker-2 >> trace.txt && cat trace.txt`
3. Run `sleep 30 && echo marker-3 >> trace.txt && cat trace.txt`
4. Run `sleep 30 && echo marker-4 >> trace.txt && cat trace.txt`

After step 4, respond with the final contents of trace.txt. Do not batch the commands into one tool call — they must be four separate Bash invocations so each commits an activity result to Dapr state.
